problem_statement.what: Developers need to find security vulnerabilities in their code before attackers do, but lack the specialized knowledge, time, and systematic methodology to do so effectively.
problem_statement.why:
Root cause (5 Whys): 1. Why needed? Developers need to find security vulnerabilities 2. Why can't they? Security review requires specialized knowledge most lack 3. Why not use tools? Tools find pattern-based issues but miss logic vulnerabilities 4. Why do logic issues require human-like review? They require understanding code INTENT, not just patterns 5. Why can Claude do this? Claude can reason about data flow, think adversarially, and do it systematically
ROOT CAUSE: Understanding code intent and reasoning about attacker behavior requires reasoning, not pattern-matching.
problem_statement.who: - Developers reviewing their own code before deployment - Security engineers conducting code audits - Teams without dedicated security staff - Anyone who needs systematic security review of source code

skill.name: deep-exploration
skill.relationship: Parent skill - provides framework methodology; security-audit specializes it for adversarial analysis

skill.name: code-review
skill.relationship: General code review - not security-focused; complements with security-specific patterns

existing_landscape.distinctiveness:
Only skill combining: 1. Adversarial mindset (think like attacker) 2. CWE-based classification (industry standard) 3. Data flow tracing methodology (source to sink) 4. Static-only analysis with honest limitations 5. Framework for Rigor (systematic coverage)
Positions as COMPLEMENT to automated tools (Semgrep, CodeQL), not replacement. Focuses on what LLMs do better: logic vulnerabilities, cross-component reasoning, context-aware analysis.

user_profile.primary_audience: Security-conscious developers and security engineers conducting code audits
user_profile.context_assumptions: - Has access to full source code - Understands basic security concepts (injection, auth, encryption) - Can read code in the target language - May not be a security specialist

requirement.description: Analyze source code to identify security vulnerabilities
Classify findings using CWE taxonomy with OWASP mapping
Use four agent perspectives: Attacker, Controls, Auditor, Design Review
Build on Framework for Rigor (shared reference)
Static code analysis only - no execution, no external tools
Findings must be actionable with remediation guidance
False positive rate must be low enough to maintain trust
requirement.source: Pre-mortem analysis - highest risk is FP fatigue

requirement.description: Explicitly state limitations (what cannot be found statically)
Every finding requires traced data flow (source to sink), not just pattern match
requirement.source: First principles lens - atomic unit of value is traced path

requirement.description: Include "Requires Runtime" confidence level for honest uncertainty
requirement.source: Constraint analysis - acknowledge static analysis limits

requirement.description: Position as complementary to automated tools, not replacement
requirement.source: Devil's advocate lens - strongest counter-argument addressed

requirement.description: Include negative findings (what was checked and found secure)
requirement.source: Inversion lens - without this, coverage claims are untestable
Constraint analysis - 4 is not a hard constraint

requirement.description: Validate skill against known-vulnerable codebases to measure recall
requirement.source: Pre-mortem - must prove methodology works empirically

pattern.name: multi-phase
pattern.rationale: Framework for Rigor requires three phases (Definition, Execution, Verification). Security audit adds Pre-Flight (Phase 0) for authorization and context gathering. This structure prevents premature conclusions and ensures systematic coverage.

phase.name: Pre-Flight
phase.purpose: Gather context, confirm authorization, set calibration level
phase.outputs: Completed pre-flight checklist, technology stack, calibration level

phase.name: Parallel Agent Deployment
phase.purpose: Explore codebase from four security perspectives simultaneously
phase.outputs: Agent findings with data flow chains, coverage matrix status

phase.name: Cross-Validation
phase.purpose: Reconcile findings across agents, identify chains, reduce false positives
phase.outputs: Validated findings with confidence levels, chain analysis

phase.name: Synthesis
phase.outputs: Complete security audit report with remediation roadmap

options.option: Light: Low-risk internal tool - 2 agents, critical paths only
Medium: Standard application - 4 agents, all dimensions
Deep: High-value target - 4 agents + multiple rounds
Confirmed: Complete source-to-sink trace, no sanitization on path
Probable: Strong pattern, sanitization may be bypassable
Possible: Suspicious pattern, requires context understanding
Requires Runtime: Dynamic dispatch, runtime config, timing-dependent

decision_point.default: Probable (conservative when evidence is strong but not complete)

evolution_analysis.score_justification: Score 8 (Solid - 2-4 years lifespan): - Principles-based: Adversarial thinking, data flow tracing, systematic coverage are timeless - Standards-aligned: CWE/OWASP are industry standards with slow evolution - Methodology-focused: Not tied to specific tools or versions - Static-only scope: Clear boundary that won't change

extension_point.purpose: Add new security dimensions as vulnerability classes emerge
Add new CWE entries as standards update
Add 5th Compliance agent for regulated industries

anti_pattern.description: Pattern matching without data flow tracing
anti_pattern.reason: Finds dangerous functions but not whether user input reaches them - produces high false positive rate
anti_pattern.alternative: Every finding requires traced source-to-sink chain documenting how untrusted data reaches sensitive operation

anti_pattern.reason: Static analysis cannot prove absence of vulnerabilities - creates false security
anti_pattern.alternative: Always state limitations; use "Requires Runtime" when verification needs execution

anti_pattern.reason: Checking boxes instead of thinking "how would I break this?" misses chain vulnerabilities
anti_pattern.alternative: Attacker agent explicitly traces exploitation paths; chain analysis in cross-validation

anti_pattern.reason: Makes remediation impossible to prioritize; loses credibility
anti_pattern.alternative: Severity requires exploitability reasoning with concrete examples

anti_pattern.reason: Django auto-escapes, Rails sanitizes by default - ignoring this produces false positives
anti_pattern.alternative: Controls agent explicitly documents framework protections; FP checklist includes framework review

anti_pattern.reason: Report without "we checked X and found it secure" sections lacks credibility
anti_pattern.alternative: Negative findings section is mandatory; documents what was checked and why it's secure

criterion.description: Find at least 80% of documented vulnerabilities when tested against known-vulnerable repositories
criterion.verification_method: Run against Juice Shop, DVWA, NodeGoat; compare findings to documented vulnerability lists

criterion.description: Every Confirmed finding has complete data flow chain (source to propagation to sink)
criterion.verification_method: Validation script checks for non-empty data_flow field on all Confirmed findings

criterion.description: False positive rate at most 10% in Confirmed findings
criterion.verification_method: Manual review of Confirmed findings against actual code; document FP rate

criterion.description: Report is understandable by intermediate-level developer (not security specialist)
criterion.verification_method: Have non-security-engineer review report; can they understand findings and remediation?

criterion.description: Coverage matrix shows 100% of dimensions examined (no empty cells)
criterion.verification_method: Validation script checks coverage matrix completeness

skill_md_structure.section: Frontmatter (YAML with name, description, license, metadata)
When to Use / When Not to Use
Phase 2: Cross-Validation with FP reduction and chain analysis
References section linking to all reference docs

reference_docs.doc: agent-prompts.md - Detailed prompts for Attacker, Controls, Auditor, Design Review agents
coverage-dimensions.md - Full definition of 10 security dimensions with checklists
cwe-reference.md - CWE Top 25 with detection patterns and OWASP mapping
severity-scoring.md - Severity criteria with concrete examples
vulnerability-format.md - Structured finding template with all required fields
verification-protocol.md - Static analysis methods and FP reduction checklist
remediation-patterns.md - Common fixes organized by CWE category

assets_needed.asset: templates/security-report.md - Complete report template with all sections
